data.title: Shell says US general licenses for exploration in Venezuela will allow it to progress with Dragon natural gas project
data.description:
Key points:
  • US licenses enable Shell's Dragon gas project progress
  • Dragon project faced delays due to US policy shifts
  • Shell aims to export gas via Atlantic LNG in Trinidad

By Curtis Williams and Shadia Nasralla

General licenses for oil and gas exploration in Venezuela issued by the U.S. this month will allow Shell to progress with its Dragon gas project, a company spokesperson told Reuters on Thursday.

The project, envisioned to produce gas from a field in Venezuelan waters with 4.5 trillion cubic feet of reserves, has been beset by starts and stops over previous years as U.S. policy toward Venezuela has shifted.

"The issuance of the recent general licenses is a positive signal, and it, indeed, allows for progress on our Dragon project," the company spokesperson told Reuters.

Shell hopes to eventually export gas from Venezuela via the Atlantic LNG facility in nearby Trinidad and Tobago. While the spokesperson said the company does not generally comment on potential dates for final investment decisions, CEO Wael Sawan recently said Shell hopes to produce gas from Venezuela's Dragon field in three years.

Shell is a 45% shareholder in Atlantic LNG, with BP also owning 45% and NGC 10%. The plant, which has the capacity to produce 12 million metric tonnes per annum, exported only 9 MT in 2025 due to gas supply shortages, data from financial firm LSEG show.

Output from Atlantic LNG was 10% of Shell's global LNG production and 15% of BP's LNG exports in 2025, according to LSEG data.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP Fall as Crypto Crisis Deepens. Why It Could Get Even Worse. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Alex Kozul-Wright

The price of Bitcoin has continued to fall as global macroeconomic uncertainty weighs on digital assets.

Bitcoin, the world's largest cryptocurrency, was down 1.7% to $66,946 over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.

The second biggest crypto Ethereum and popular altcoin XRP also dropped, by 2.3% and 4.4%, respectively.

"No major catalyst looks set to push [cryptocurrencies] higher," Gerry O'Shea, head of market insights at Hashdex, told Barron's Wednesday.

O'Shea noted that global economic uncertainty looks set to pin major cryptocurrencies within a falling range and expects the price slide to continue.

"The market is dealing with uncertainty around Fed policy," O'Shea said. Adding: " Christine Lagarde may also be stepping down [as head of the ECB]."

The upshot is that, in the absence of any clear price drivers, he thinks Bitcoin could fall below $65,000.

Weekly jobs data due 8:30 a.m. Eastern time Thursday will offer a steer on the U.S. labor market. Together with Friday's personal consumption expenditures data, the numbers could influence the Federal Reserve's path on monetary policy.

If economic conditions prove softer than expected, near-term Fed rate cuts could become more likely — which would push investors to riskier assets such as cryptocurrencies.

The Wall Street Journal reported that Fed officials want to see further progress on inflation before considering more interest-rate cuts.

Write to Alex Kozul-Wright at alexander.kozul-wright@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: American Homes 4 Rent Q4 Sales Increase
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - American Homes 4 Rent (AMH) revealed earnings for fourth quarter of $123.80 million

The company's bottom line totaled $123.80 million, or $0.33 per share. This compares with $123.23 million, or $0.33 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 4.2% to $454.99 million from $436.59 million last year.

American Homes 4 Rent earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: $123.80 Mln. vs. $123.23 Mln. last year.

-EPS: $0.33 vs. $0.33 last year.

-Revenue: $454.99 Mln vs. $436.59 Mln last year.

data.title: CTO Realty Growth swings to net income in Q4, occupancy hits record in 2025
data.description:
Overview

  • Shopping center operator swings to net income in Q4 from a loss a year ago

  • Core FFO and AFFO per share for Q4 showed growth

  • At year-end, the company achieved record leased occupancy and substantial leasing activity

Outlook

  • CTO Realty Growth expects 2026 Core FFO per share between $1.98 and $2.03

  • Company forecasts 2026 AFFO per share between $2.11 and $2.16

  • CTO Realty Growth anticipates 2026 investment volume of $100 mln to $200 mln

Result Drivers

  • RECORD LEASING ACTIVITY - CTO executed 167,000 sq ft of comparable retail leases at a 31% cash rent spread increase in Q4, contributing to a record 592,000 sq ft for the year

  • HIGH OCCUPANCY - Leased occupancy reached a record high of 95.9% at year-end, up 250 basis points from the previous year

  • INVESTMENT ACTIVITY - Acquired Pompano Citi Centre for $65.2 mln and sold Shops at Legacy North for $78 mln in Q4
